title: Remove user-manual-rename guard and tmux_window_title column
description: The tmux_window_title column and user-manual-rename guard in applyTmuxTitle are now counterproductive. With the Rename Session feature, users have an explicit way to set window titles. The guard blocks title updates when the tmux window name drifts (e.g., automatic-rename). Remove the column, guard, and all related code. Design: docs/plans/2026-03-01-remove-tmux-window-title-guard-design.md
close_reason: Removed tmux_window_title column usage and user-manual-rename guard. Migration zeroes column, all Go code references removed, architecture docs updated, full build passes.

title: Debug SESSION column showing -- in mission resume picker
description:
After aligning SESSION column with tmux title priority chain (commit f8ec6a4), some missions show -- in the mission resume fzf picker despite having agenc_custom_title set via rename.

Findings so far:
- Server IS running new code (version 7d8d776, which includes enrichMissionWithSessionTitle)
- Server correctly returns resolved_session_title='testing rename' for mission eadf4851
- That mission has tmux_pane=26, so it's RUNNING not STOPPED — it wouldn't appear in the resume picker (which filters to stopped missions only)
- Need to check which mission actually shows -- in the picker
- Also reverted the checkServerVersion-in-serverClient change (user said don't do that)
- The revert of checkServerVersion still needs to be committed

Next steps:
1. Commit the revert of checkServerVersion from serverClient
2. Query all missions to see which stopped ones have empty resolved_session_title
3. Check if those missions have sessions in the sessions table
4. The issue may be that stopped missions never had the session scanner create sessions for them (scanner only runs for missions with tmux panes in the pool)
